content:
Summary: Implemented the chat thread ordering fix so streamed assistant/tool content now renders top-to-bottom in chronological order. The live assistant stream in useChat now splits into separate assistant segments around tool calls, preventing post-tool deltas from being appended to the pre-tool placeholder bubble. The task card was moved to done after the change was applied.

Facts:
1. [finished] The chat stream now tracks the active assistant message id in src/lib/chat/useChat.ts so tool-call boundaries can split assistant output into separate ordered segments.
2. [finished] Tool-call cards are still appended in sequence, but an empty assistant placeholder is removed when a tool call arrives before any streamed text, preventing a blank bubble above the tool card.
3. [finished] If a delta arrives after a tool call, useChat creates a new assistant message segment instead of appending to the earlier pre-tool assistant bubble.
4. [finished] The task card b1dca4d5-420e-459f-9dc2-2522f3237bbd was moved from in-progress to done after the ordering fix was implemented.
5. [finished] Only src/lib/chat/useChat.ts was modified for this ordering fix; persisted history ordering and the chat API route were left unchanged.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/chat/useChat.ts — Implemented the streaming message segmentation fix so assistant output is split around tool calls and rendered chronologically in the message list. | Notes: Added active assistant message tracking, tool-boundary placeholder removal, new assistant segment creation after tool calls, and safer error fallback handling.

The live assistant stream in useChat now splits into separate assistant segments around tool calls, preventing post-tool deltas from being appended to the pre-tool placeholder bubble. The task card was moved to done after the change was applied.

content:
Summary: Implemented workspace-aware AI tools for the OpenAI SDK chat flow. The backend now exposes four server-side workspace tools (list artifacts, global search, keyword location, and line-range reads) backed by the Supabase workspace file repository, and the chat route streams tool events plus assistant deltas through SSE. The client now renders tool call cards with stable ids and can jump to artifacts/ranges from tool invocations.

Facts:
1. [finished] src/lib/ai/workspace-tools.ts now defines OpenAI tool schemas and prepares workspace-bound tool invocations with Zod validation.
2. [finished] src/lib/workspace-files/search.ts now provides shared search helpers for artifact listing, global search, keyword location, and line-range reads.
3. [finished] src/lib/chat/openai.ts now orchestrates OpenAI chat completions with tool calling, streaming text deltas and workspace tool events back to the route.
4. [finished] src/app/api/chat/route.ts now forwards assistant delta, tool_call, and tool_result SSE events while persisting the final assistant reply.
5. [finished] src/lib/chat/useChat.ts and src/components/CommandCenter/types.ts now track tool call ids and update tool card status in the client thread.
6. [finished] src/app/page.tsx now resolves tool clicks by artifact id first, then by file name, so workspace tool cards can open the correct document.

Affected files:
1. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/search.ts — Added shared workspace file search and read helpers that power all four AI tools without duplicating repository logic. | Notes: Includes listing, global search, keyword location, and line-range extraction helpers.
2. [created] src/lib/ai/workspace-tools.ts — Added the OpenAI tool schemas and workspace-bound invocation preparation layer required for model tool calling. | Notes: Validates tool arguments with Zod and returns structured descriptors for UI events.
3. [modified] src/lib/chat/openai.ts — Replaced the old unused OpenAI helper with the new tool-aware chat orchestration flow using the OpenAI SDK. | Notes: Handles streamed deltas, tool call loops, and tool result messages.
4. [modified] src/app/api/chat/route.ts — Wired the HTTP chat endpoint to the new OpenAI tool orchestration and SSE event stream for tool calls and tool results. | Notes: Still persists user and assistant messages to Supabase.
5. [modified] src/lib/chat/useChat.ts — Updated the client chat hook to render tool call lifecycle events and keep tool statuses synchronized in the conversation thread. | Notes: Now tracks tool call ids from the SSE stream.
6. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter/types.ts — Expanded the tool call type so client state can identify and update individual tool invocations. | Notes: Added artifactId to support document navigation from tool cards.
7. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter/MessageBubble.tsx — Adjusted the live-message rendering so completed tool call cards do not keep showing the streaming loading indicator. | Notes: Only plain assistant placeholders now render the thinking dots.
8. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Updated tool call navigation to resolve artifacts by id first and then by name, enabling precise file jumps from tool cards. | Notes: Works with read/range tool results.
9. [modified] package.json — Declared zod as a direct dependency because the new tool layer uses Zod schemas at runtime and type validation boundaries. | Notes: Version added matches the lockfile entry already present in the workspace.
10. [modified] package-lock.json — Kept the root dependency manifest aligned with package.json after adding the direct zod dependency. | Notes: Only the root dependency list was updated; the existing zod package entry already existed in the lockfile.

The client now renders tool call cards with stable ids and can jump to artifacts/ranges from tool invocations.

content:
Summary: Updated workspace document ingestion so extracted PDF/DOCX/TXT/MD content now drops leading and trailing blank lines before storing both the numbered line array and the concatenated extracted text. This keeps the downstream contract intact while removing empty boundary lines from uploaded documents.

Facts:
1. [finished] The workspace ingestion pipeline normalizes extracted document text in src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts before returning ParsedWorkspaceFile.
2. [finished] Leading and trailing empty lines are now trimmed from extracted text by slicing the sanitized line array, while internal blank lines are preserved.
3. [finished] The stored extracted_text value is rebuilt from the trimmed extracted_lines array so the saved text and line array stay aligned.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Implemented trimming of leading and trailing empty lines during workspace document ingestion and rebuilt stored extracted text from the trimmed lines. | Notes: No other files were changed for this fix.

This keeps the downstream contract intact while removing empty boundary lines from uploaded documents.

content:
Summary: Switched workspace file ingestion from hand-rolled parsing to dedicated PDF/DOCX libraries. The upload path now targets @cedrugs/pdf-parse for PDFs and mammoth for DOCX, while continuing to normalize extracted text into numbered lines for the viewer and future AI file-reading tool. Added ambient declarations for the new modules and updated package.json dependencies accordingly.

Facts:
1. [finished] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts now uses dedicated parsers for PDF and DOCX instead of the previous custom byte/regex extraction logic.
2. [finished] package.json now includes @cedrugs/pdf-parse and mammoth so the upload pipeline can extract readable text from PDF and DOCX uploads.
3. [finished] Ambient module declarations were added under src/types for mammoth and @cedrugs/pdf-parse to keep the TypeScript boundary explicit.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Replaced the custom PDF/DOCX extraction heuristics with proper parser-based extraction so uploads yield readable text lines. | Notes: Keeps sanitization and line normalization after extraction.
2. [modified] package.json — Added the new parser dependencies required for readable PDF and DOCX extraction. | Notes: Dependencies need to be installed into node_modules.
3. [created] src/types/mammoth.d.ts — Provides an explicit TypeScript contract for the mammoth parser import used by the ingestion layer. | Notes: Keeps the module boundary typed.
4. [created] src/types/cedrugs-pdf-parse.d.ts — Provides an explicit TypeScript contract for the PDF parser import used by the ingestion layer. | Notes: Keeps the module boundary typed.

Added ambient declarations for the new modules and updated package.json dependencies accordingly.

content:
Summary: Fixed the workspace file upload failure caused by malformed Unicode reaching Supabase. The ingestion pipeline now sanitizes extracted and inserted text to remove NULs and lone surrogates before persistence, which should prevent the Postgres Unicode escape error during file uploads.

Facts:
1. [finished] The upload path uses src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts to parse txt, md, pdf, and docx files into extracted text and line arrays before saving them.
2. [finished] The database insert path in src/lib/workspace-files/repository.ts now sanitizes workspace file fields before calling Supabase, providing a second safety layer against malformed Unicode.
3. [finished] The reported error message was '[workspace-files] createWorkspaceFile failed: unsupported Unicode escape sequence', which pointed to a persistence-boundary issue rather than a UI rendering problem.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Added Unicode normalization and sanitization for extracted file text, line arrays, and upload metadata before persistence. | Notes: Removes NUL characters and lone surrogates.
2. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/repository.ts — Added defensive sanitization before the Supabase insert to prevent malformed Unicode from reaching the database. | Notes: Provides a second validation layer at persistence time.

The ingestion pipeline now sanitizes extracted and inserted text to remove NULs and lone surrogates before persistence, which should prevent the Postgres Unicode escape error during file uploads.

content:
Summary: Implemented workspace file upload and text extraction flow. The workspace panel now exposes a real upload button and empty-state CTA, uploads txt/md/pdf/docx through a workspace-scoped API route, extracts normalized line-based text on the server, persists uploaded file metadata and extracted lines in Supabase, and keeps a repository hook ready for future AI file-reading tools.

Facts:
1. [finished] The workspace panel in src/app/page.tsx now loads persisted workspace files, shows their count in the ArtifactViewer header, and uploads new files through a hidden file input using the supported workspace file accept list.
2. [finished] A new Supabase migration sqls/002_workspace_files.sql adds the workspace_files table with extracted_text, extracted_lines, line_count, processing_status, timestamps, and RLS enabled.
3. [finished] src/lib/supabase/types.ts now includes WorkspaceFileRow, WorkspaceFileInsert, and the workspace_files table relationship so client and server code share explicit contracts.
4. [finished] Server-side ingestion and persistence live under src/lib/workspace-files/ with validation, document extraction, repository helpers, and a line-range helper for future AI file-reading tools.
5. [finished] A new workspace-scoped API route at src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/files/route.ts handles GET listing and POST multipart uploads, validates workspace existence, parses the upload, and stores the normalized result.

Affected files:
1. [created] sqls/002_workspace_files.sql — Adds the new Supabase table and related indexes/triggers needed to persist uploaded files and extracted lines. | Notes: Includes RLS enablement and updated_at trigger.
2. [modified] src/lib/supabase/types.ts — Extends the generated-style database contract with workspace file row, insert, and relationship types. | Notes: Keeps client/server type usage explicit.
3. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/validation.ts — Centralizes file-type allowlisting, size limits, and accept-string generation for upload validation. | Notes: Shared by client and server code.
4. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Implements server-side extraction and normalization for txt, md, pdf, and docx files into line arrays. | Notes: Uses built-in parsing logic rather than external packages.
5. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/repository.ts — Provides Supabase persistence and retrieval helpers for uploaded workspace files, plus a reusable line-range helper for future AI reads. | Notes: Server-side data access only.
6. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/client.ts — Wraps the workspace file API for the client-side page orchestration layer. | Notes: Handles fetch, upload, and response validation.
7. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/mappers.ts — Converts persisted file rows into ArtifactViewer artifacts so uploaded files render with the existing line-based viewer. | Notes: Keeps UI mapping separate from data access.
8. [created] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/files/route.ts — Exposes workspace-scoped GET and POST endpoints for listing and uploading extracted files. | Notes: Validates workspace existence and multipart uploads.
9. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/index.tsx — Adds the workspace file header upload control to the left panel while keeping the viewer composition-focused. | Notes: Now accepts fileCount and onUploadFiles props.
10. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/EmptyWorkspace.tsx — Reworks the empty state into a file upload call to action with the supported document formats. | Notes: Removed the old sample deploy wording.
11. [created] src/components/ArtifactViewer/WorkspacePanelHeader.tsx — Provides the new top-of-panel upload entry point and file count display without bloating the viewer entry file. | Notes: Uses neutral styling consistent with the app.
12. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Orchestrates workspace file loading, upload actions, hidden file input handling, and artifact state synchronization. | Notes: Keeps the page as the composition layer and delegates extraction/persistence to helpers.

The workspace panel now exposes a real upload button and empty-state CTA, uploads txt/md/pdf/docx through a workspace-scoped API route, extracts normalized line-based text on the server, persists uploaded file metadata and extracted lines in Supabase, and keeps a repository hook ready for future AI file-reading tools.

content:
Summary: User wants the left workspace panel (ArtifactViewer area) to support uploading files, extract text from txt/md/pdf/docx into line-based content, persist the extracted content in Supabase, and leave a clean hook for a future AI file-reading tool. I inspected the workspace page, ArtifactViewer, chat input, workspace repository, Supabase schema/types, and existing API patterns to shape the plan.

Facts:
1. [finished] The main workspace UI is in src/app/page.tsx, where the left panel is rendered by src/components/ArtifactViewer/index.tsx and currently shows sample artifacts rather than real uploads.
2. [finished] The current project has Supabase-backed workspaces, conversations, and messages, but no file-upload or document-ingestion tables or routes yet.
3. [finished] The existing UI already includes an unused attachment affordance in src/components/CommandCenter/ChatInput.tsx, but there is no FileReader or upload pipeline implemented.
4. [finished] The initial Supabase schema lives in sqls/001_initial_schema.sql and src/lib/supabase/types.ts must stay synchronized with any new tables added for uploaded files.

Affected files:
1. [inspected] src/app/page.tsx — This is the top-level workspace composition and controls the left ArtifactViewer panel where upload support should be integrated. | Notes: Contains current workspace state, sample artifacts, and panel layout.
2. [inspected] src/components/ArtifactViewer/index.tsx — This is the workspace panel component that will likely host upload/dropzone and file preview behavior. | Notes: Currently only renders tabs, content, and an empty state CTA.
3. [inspected] src/components/ArtifactViewer/EmptyWorkspace.tsx — This empty state is the most likely UI entry point for adding upload affordances and supported-format guidance. | Notes: Currently only offers a sample deploy button.
4. [inspected] src/components/CommandCenter/ChatInput.tsx — Contains an existing attachment button that may inform or share UX patterns for file input styling. | Notes: No file upload logic exists yet.
5. [inspected] src/lib/workspace/repository.ts — Shows the established Supabase repository pattern to mirror for workspace file persistence. | Notes: Useful for future repository separation.
6. [inspected] src/lib/chat/repository.ts — Provides the repository structure and validation/error-handling style to follow for new file ingestion helpers. | Notes: Good reference for server-side data access conventions.
7. [inspected] src/lib/supabase/types.ts — This file will need to be extended to include any new file/document tables introduced by the migration. | Notes: Must stay in sync with Supabase schema.
8. [inspected] sqls/001_initial_schema.sql — Baseline migration shows current tables and trigger/RLS patterns to extend for uploaded files. | Notes: New migration should follow the same style.
9. [inspected] src/app/api/workspaces/route.ts — Defines the existing API style for workspace list/create routes, which new file routes should match. | Notes: Validates JSON handling and error responses.
10. [inspected] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/chat/route.ts — Shows the pattern for workspace-scoped API routes that the file ingestion route should mirror. | Notes: Useful for params handling and 404 behavior.

metadata.summary: User wants the left workspace panel (ArtifactViewer area) to support uploading files, extract text from txt/md/pdf/docx into line-based content, persist the extracted content in Supabase, and leave a clean hook for a future AI file-reading tool. I inspected the workspace page, ArtifactViewer, chat input, workspace repository, Supabase schema/types, and existing API patterns to shape the plan.

content:
Summary: Implemented a Supabase-backed workspace and chat flow. The app now loads real workspaces from Supabase, allows creating a workspace through a modal opened from a plus button in the sidebar and mobile header, and loads workspace-specific chat history through a workspace chat API. The chat send path now requires a valid workspace, validates that conversations belong to the selected workspace, and falls back to creating a conversation inside that workspace when needed. No SQL migration changes were required because the existing workspaces/conversations/messages schema already supported the flow.

Facts:
1. [finished] The left sidebar now renders real workspace rows from Supabase instead of hardcoded mock workspaces and includes a create-workspace plus button plus an empty-state CTA when no workspaces exist.
2. [finished] Workspace creation is handled through a new modal component and the client posts to a new /api/workspaces route, which validates the name and creates a workspace row in Supabase.
3. [finished] Workspace chat history is loaded through a new /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/chat route and the useChat hook now fetches and maps persisted messages whenever the active workspace changes.
4. [finished] The chat send API now requires a real workspaceId, validates that any provided conversation belongs to the active workspace, and creates a new workspace conversation if needed.
5. [finished] No changes were made to sqls/001_initial_schema.sql because the existing schema already supported workspaces, conversations, and messages for workspace-scoped chats.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Replaced mock workspace state with Supabase-loaded workspaces, added workspace creation flow, and wired the page to the new chat loader and modal. | Notes: Main UI orchestrator for the feature.
2. [modified] src/components/Sidebar.tsx — Sidebar now shows real workspace rows, adds a plus button for creating workspaces, and handles the empty-workspace state. | Notes: Desktop navigation entrypoint.
3. [created] src/components/CreateWorkspaceModal/index.tsx — New modal component for creating workspaces from the sidebar/mobile header CTA with validation and inline error handling. | Notes: Keeps workspace creation UI isolated.
4. [modified] src/lib/chat/useChat.ts — Hook now loads persisted workspace chat history from Supabase before streaming new messages and blocks sends while history is loading. | Notes: Client-side chat state and streaming logic.
5. [created] src/app/api/workspaces/route.ts — New API route to list workspaces and create a new workspace with validation. | Notes: Server boundary for workspace CRUD.
6. [created] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/chat/route.ts — New API route to return the latest conversation and persisted messages for a workspace. | Notes: Server boundary for workspace-specific chat loading.
7. [modified] src/app/api/chat/route.ts — Chat streaming route now enforces valid workspace ownership for conversations and no longer falls back to the old auto-created default workspace flow. | Notes: Prevents chats from escaping the selected workspace.
8. [modified] src/lib/workspace/repository.ts — Added a listWorkspaces helper used by the new workspace listing API. | Notes: Keeps workspace data access centralized.
9. [modified] src/lib/chat/repository.ts — Added workspace conversation listing helpers used by the workspace chat loader. | Notes: Supports selecting the active workspace conversation.
10. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter/index.tsx — Added a neutral empty-state panel so the chat area still has a useful workspace-ready state when there are no messages. | Notes: Improves UX for newly created or empty workspaces.
11. [inspected] sqls/001_initial_schema.sql — Verified the existing schema already contained workspaces, conversations, and messages, so no migration was needed for this feature. | Notes: No file changes required.

The chat send path now requires a valid workspace, validates that conversations belong to the selected workspace, and falls back to creating a conversation inside that workspace when needed. content:
Summary: Adjusted the chat UI layout so the command center message list scrolls independently and the composer stays attached to the bottom without a separator line. The change was made primarily in the CommandCenter component, with a small parent layout tweak in the page to ensure the flex column can shrink correctly.

Facts:
1. [finished] The command center message list is now wrapped in a min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto container so only the message area scrolls.
2. [finished] The composer area no longer renders a top divider border above the input, removing the separator line between messages and the composer.
3. [finished] The page layout now applies min-h-0 to the chat panel container so the scrollable message region can size correctly inside the full-height split view.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\canvas-ai\src\components\CommandCenter.tsx — Contains the message list and composer layout; updated to make the message list scrollable and remove the separator above the composer. | Notes: Added flex sizing and overflow handling to the message list wrapper and removed the composer top border.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\canvas-ai\src\app\page.tsx — Provides the chat panel container whose flex sizing must allow the internal scroll area to shrink properly. | Notes: Added min-h-0 to the chat panel wrapper and its sibling split panel wrapper.

The change was made primarily in the CommandCenter component, with a small parent layout tweak in the page to ensure the flex column can shrink correctly.

content:
Summary: Implemented a shared workspace settings system so the settings modal now controls real UI behavior across the app. The settings state was lifted to the page and threaded into the sidebar, artifact viewer, command center, and modal. Theme now updates the overall shell and panel styling, density changes spacing, font size adjusts artifact text/table rendering, line numbers can be toggled in the viewer, and auto-scroll can be disabled in the chat panel. The settings modal was also kept as a dedicated overlay component, with the About section removed earlier and the modal now reflecting the shared theme state.

Facts:
1. [finished] Workspace settings are now centralized in src/components/workspaceSettings.ts and shared across the page, sidebar, artifact viewer, command center, and settings modal.
2. [finished] Theme changes now affect the main app shell, mobile header, sidebar, settings modal, artifact viewer, and command center styling.
3. [finished] Density settings now change spacing in the artifact viewer, command center, and modal content layout.
4. [finished] Font size now controls artifact viewer text sizing for both text files and sheet rendering.
5. [finished] Line numbers can now be toggled in the artifact viewer and auto-scroll can be turned on or off in the command center.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Lifted settings state to the workspace page and passed shared settings into the sidebar, artifact viewer, and command center. | Notes: Also updated the mobile shell to reflect theme changes.
2. [modified] src/components/SettingsModal.tsx — Converted modal controls to receive shared settings state and dispatch updates instead of maintaining isolated local state. | Notes: The modal now reflects the app theme and density state.
3. [modified] src/components/Sidebar.tsx — Sidebar now passes the shared settings state into the modal so theme changes and control updates can affect the whole app. | Notes: The settings button still opens the modal.
4. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer.tsx — Artifact rendering now responds to shared line-number, font-size, theme, and density settings. | Notes: Text and sheet rendering both use the shared settings contract.
5. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter.tsx — Chat panel now respects shared theme, density, and auto-scroll settings. | Notes: Auto-scroll can be toggled off from the modal.
6. [created] src/components/workspaceSettings.ts — Introduced a shared typed settings contract to avoid duplicating setting shapes and to keep state updates explicit. | Notes: Defines the default workspace settings and updater type.

metadata.summary: Implemented a shared workspace settings system so the settings modal now controls real UI behavior across the app. The settings state was lifted to the page and threaded into the sidebar, artifact viewer, command center, and modal. Theme now updates the overall shell and panel styling, density changes spacing, font size adjusts artifact text/table rendering, line numbers can be toggled in the viewer, and auto-scroll can be disabled in the chat panel. The settings modal was also kept as a dedicated overlay component, with the About section removed earlier and the modal now reflecting the shared theme state.
